meta.keywords: emfont, webfont, Chinese fonts, open source
meta.author: Elvis Mao

site.aboutIntro1: emfont is a free, easy-to-use CJK webfont service. Add two lines of JavaScript to your site, apply a class to an element, and rich typefaces are ready to use. It helps websites, portfolios, and blogs gain a stronger visual voice without heavy setup.
site.aboutIntro2: emfont currently offers more than 90 open-source Chinese typefaces. It is lightweight, stable, and removes the usual setup and licensing friction so designers and developers can focus on the work itself.
site.aboutThanksAfter: for providing fast and stable computing resources in Taiwan. Their support helps emfont keep serving everyone. Most importantly, every user keeps this mission alive: letting designers work with Chinese type freely and creatively.
site.aboutSupportBefore: If you like this open-source project, please leave a star on
site.aboutSupportAfter: . If you have questions or want to help build emfont, join our
site.marquee1: Use GenYo Gothic instead of Microsoft JhengHei
site.marquee2: Use GenYo Mincho instead of PMingLiU
site.marquee3: Use jf open Huninn instead of Source Han Sans
site.marquee4: Use Mantou Sans instead of Roboto
site.marquee5: Use ChenYu LuoYan instead of LiHei Pro
site.marquee6: Use Chill Bitmap instead of DFKai-SB

site.firstTimeDescriptionBefore: emfont is a free webfont service that automatically compresses and optimizes font files. You can
site.readDocs: read the docs to get started
site.footerDescription: Text gives a website its soul, its ideas, and its visual character. Chinese fonts are often large and expensive to serve, so free Chinese webfont services are rare. emfont was created to fill that gap by collecting open-source fonts and giving developers and designers a practical Chinese webfont solution.
